title: toxicodendron lanceum
intro: Toxicodendron lanceum, also known as the Japanese poison oak or lacquer tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Toxicodendron lanceum include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging effects. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and its extracts are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
